It handles protocol and transport details that should not be reimplemented in 1C business code:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eHTTP/SSE transport\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eJSON-RPC request/response lifecycle\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eMCP session management\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eauthentication, origin validation, and rate limiting\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003epagination, notifications, and progress streaming\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003econverting 1C responses into valid MCP replies\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e1C responsibilities\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-1c-responsibilities\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 1C responsibilities\" href=\"#1c-responsibilities\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe 1C side owns business logic. A 1C developer should work at the level of tools, resources, and prompts, not at the level of MCP internals:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003edescribe a tool/resource/prompt in BSL\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eregister it in the component\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ehandle the incoming call in 1C\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003erun any required client-side or server-side 1C logic\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ereturn the result or send progress updates\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhy the project is designed this way\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-why-the-project-is-designed-this-way\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Why the project is designed this way\" href=\"#why-the-project-is-designed-this-way\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe goal is to let a 1C developer publish almost any 1C functionality through MCP without having to understand HTTP, JSON-RPC, SSE, session handling, or MCP message formatting.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn other words:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ethe component knows how to be an MCP server\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e1C knows what the tool actually does\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis keeps the integration point simple. To add new functionality, a 1C developer does not need to change the native transport layer. They only add or update 1C definitions and handlers.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eMental model for a 1C developer\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-mental-model-for-a-1c-developer\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Mental model for a 1C developer\" href=\"#mental-model-for-a-1c-developer\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFrom the 1C side, the workflow is intentionally simple:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDefine the MCP object in BSL.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eRegister it in the component.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eReceive the request through \u003ccode\u003eExternalEvent\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eExecute arbitrary 1C logic.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eReturn the final result, and optionally send progress while the operation is running.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis means the project is not a fixed set of built-in utilities. It is an MCP transport and protocol layer for 1C, with the actual application behavior defined in 1C code.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eKey Features\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-key-features\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Key Features\" 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFull MCP protocol support\u003c/strong\u003e — tools, resources, prompts with \u003ccode\u003elistChanged\u003c/code\u003e notifications\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eStreamable HTTP transport\u003c/strong\u003e — \u003ccode\u003ePOST /mcp\u003c/code\u003e for requests, \u003ccode\u003eGET /mcp\u003c/code\u003e for SSE notification stream\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSession management\u003c/strong\u003e — \u003ccode\u003eMcp-Session-Id\u003c/code\u003e header, UUID v4 sessions per spec\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSecurity\u003c/strong\u003e — Origin validation (DNS rebinding protection), Bearer token auth, rate limiting\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eProgress streaming\u003c/strong\u003e — SSE-based progress notifications for long-running operations\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePagination\u003c/strong\u003e — cursor-based pagination for \u003ccode\u003etools/list\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eresources/list\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eprompts/list\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTool annotations\u003c/strong\u003e — \u003ccode\u003ereadOnlyHint\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003edestructiveHint\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eidempotentHint\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eopenWorldHint\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOutput schemas\u003c/strong\u003e — typed response contracts for tool results\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDynamic registration\u003c/strong\u003e — register/update tools, resources, prompts at runtime from 1C\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBuilt-in semantic search (RAG)\u003c/strong\u003e — optional \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e tools backed by a Rust search core (\u003ccode\u003ercore\u003c/code\u003e) with dense/keyword/hybrid retrieval (see \u003ca href=\"#search-subsystem-rag\"\u003eSearch Subsystem\u003c/a\u003e)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"┌─────────────────┐     HTTP/SSE      ┌──────────────────┐    ExternalEvent   ┌──────────────────┐\n│   MCP Client    │◄─────────────────►│   Native DLL     │◄──────────────────►│  1C:Enterprise   │\n│  (VS Code, etc) │   POST/GET /mcp   │  (HttpServer)    │   ToolCall, etc.   │  (BSL Module)    │\n└─────────────────┘                   └──────────────────┘                    └──────────────────┘\"\u003e\u003cpre class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e┌─────────────────┐     HTTP/SSE      ┌──────────────────┐    ExternalEvent   ┌──────────────────┐\n│   MCP Client    │◄─────────────────►│   Native DLL     │◄──────────────────►│  1C:Enterprise   │\n│  (VS Code, etc) │   POST/GET /mcp   │  (HttpServer)    │   ToolCall, etc.   │  (BSL Module)    │\n└─────────────────┘                   └──────────────────┘                    └──────────────────┘\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eA 1C form loads the native add-in and starts the HTTP server.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eAn MCP client connects to \u003ccode\u003ehttp://localhost:PORT/mcp\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe native component handles protocol-level messages (initialize, tools/list, etc.).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eBusiness logic requests (tools/call, resources/read, prompts/get) are forwarded to 1C via \u003ccode\u003eExternalEvent\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe 1C module processes the request and sends results back through \u003ccode\u003eSendResponse\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe DLL wraps the result in a JSON-RPC response and returns it to the client.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cdiv The MCP server\n\u003cstrong\u003estarts automatically on open\u003c/strong\u003e (attaches the component, registers the tool /\nresource / prompt catalogs, and begins listening on port \u003ccode\u003e8888\u003c/code\u003e by default).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eTo restart it on a different port, set the port field and click \u003cstrong\u003eConnect\u003c/strong\u003e.\n(The \u003ccode\u003eConnect\u003c/code\u003e button remains available for manual control.)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eConfigure your MCP client to connect to \u003ccode\u003ehttp://localhost:PORT/mcp\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e5. The entered value is masked as a password.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eImportant:\u003c/strong\u003e The token in VS Code must match the value set on the 1C side. If the server has no token configured (empty string), authentication is disabled and no \u003ccode\u003eheaders\u003c/code\u003e are needed. If a token is set on the server but VS Code sends no \u003ccode\u003eAuthorization\u003c/code\u003e header, the server responds with HTTP 401 and VS Code may try to start an OAuth flow — this is not supported; use the \u003ccode\u003eheaders\u003c/code\u003e approach above instead.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eHow to Build Your Own MCP Server from 1C\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-how-to-build-your-own-mcp-server-from-1c\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: How to Build Your Own MCP Server from 1C\" href=\"#how-to-build-your-own-mcp-server-from-1c\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe reference data processor (\u003ccode\u003ehttp-1c-dp\u003c/code\u003e) is a working example. data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"// Create a tool definition\nTool = NewTool(\u0026quot;myTool\u0026quot;, \u0026quot;Description of what this tool does\u0026quot;);\nAddToolParam(Tool, \u0026quot;paramName\u0026quot;, \u0026quot;string\u0026quot;, \u0026quot;Parameter description\u0026quot;);\nAddToolAnnotations(Tool, True);  // readOnly, safe\n\n// Define output schema (optional, description\u0026quot;);\nSetToolOutputSchema(Tool, Schema);\n\n// Register all tools\nTools = New Array;\nTools.Add(Tool);\nAwait token\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eClient header\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eResult\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEmpty my-secret\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRequest accepted\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003e\"my-secret\"\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eMissing or wrong token\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eHTTP 401 Unauthorized\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eChanging the token at runtime:\u003c/strong\u003e You can set or clear \u003ccode\u003eAuthToken\u003c/code\u003e while the server is running. The change takes effect immediately for all new requests — no restart needed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eVS Code note:\u003c/strong\u003e If the server returns 401, VS Code may attempt an OAuth 2.0 PKCE authorization flow (redirecting to \u003ccode\u003e/authorize\u003c/code\u003e). This is \u003cstrong\u003enot supported\u003c/strong\u003e by the component. Always configure the token in \u003ccode\u003e.vscode/mcp.json\u003c/code\u003e via the \u003ccode\u003eheaders\u003c/code\u003e field (see \u003ca href=\"#5-vs-code-configuration\"\u003eVS Code configuration\u003c/a\u003e above).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe component also enforces:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOrigin validation\u003c/strong\u003e — only requests from \u003ccode\u003elocalhost\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003e127.0.0.1\u003c/code\u003e / VS Code origins are accepted\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRate limiting\u003c/strong\u003e — token-bucket algorithm (60 burst, 20/sec)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSession management\u003c/strong\u003e — \u003ccode\u003eMcp-Session-Id\u003c/code\u003e assigned on initialize, validated on subsequent requests. A request presenting an \u003cstrong\u003eunknown\u003c/strong\u003e session id is not rejected with 404 — the session is transparently \u003cstrong\u003eresurrected\u003c/strong\u003e under the presented id, so a server restart does not strand connected MCP clients that never re-initialize (logged as \u003ccode\u003eMCP: unknown session ... resurrected (server restart?)\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eNative Component API\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-native-component-api\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Native Component API\" href=\"#native-component-api\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eMethods exposed to 1C (English / Russian names). \u003ccode\u003eTimeout\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eTools\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eResources\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ePrompts\u003c/code\u003e, These still exist, but reading or writing an AddIn property is a\n\u003cstrong\u003esynchronous\u003c/strong\u003e platform call.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIn an \u003cstrong\u003easync-only infobase\u003c/strong\u003e — i.e. one where \u003cem\u003e\"synchronous extension and\nadd-in calls\"\u003c/em\u003e are disabled (the modern 1C default) — every \u003ccode\u003eComponent.Prop = …\u003c/code\u003e\nassignment and every \u003ccode\u003e… = Component.Prop\u003c/code\u003e read throws\n\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eCannot call synchronous methods on the client!\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e. 1C provides \u003cstrong\u003eno async\nproperty accessors\u003c/strong\u003e (there is no \u003ccode\u003eBeginSetProperty\u003c/code\u003e), so configuration cannot\ngo through properties at all in that mode.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThat is why all configuration is funneled through the \u003cstrong\u003easync \u003ccode\u003eApplyConfig\u003c/code\u003e\nmethod\u003c/strong\u003e and all status reads through the \u003cstrong\u003easync \u003ccode\u003eGetStatus\u003c/code\u003e function\u003c/strong\u003e. The\nreference form (\u003ccode\u003eModule.bsl\u003c/code\u003e) uses only these — it never touches a property on\nthe client. The properties are retained for backward compatibility and for\ninfobases that still allow synchronous calls.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eConfig field (in \u003ccode\u003eApplyConfig\u003c/code\u003e JSON)\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eEquivalent property\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eNotes\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003elogging_enabled\u003c/code\u003e (bool)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eLoggingEnabled\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eruntime logging on/off\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003elog_path\u003c/code\u003e (string)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eLogPath\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003elog file path (empty → default)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003etimeout\u003c/code\u003e seconds\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003etools_json\u003c/code\u003e tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSearch Subsystem (RAG)\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-search-subsystem-rag\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Search Subsystem (RAG)\" href=\"#search-subsystem-rag\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe component ships with an optional semantic-search subsystem so a 1C MCP server can index its own content (documentation, code, catalog descriptions, logs, QA scenarios, master data) and let AI clients search it \u003cstrong\u003eby meaning\u003c/strong\u003e. The retrieval engine is a small Rust core, crate \u003ccode\u003ercore\u003c/code\u003e, that runs entirely in-process inside the 1C session.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhy this exists\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-why-this-exists\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Why this exists\" href=\"#why-this-exists\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAn MCP server already lets an AI client \u003cem\u003eact\u003c/em\u003e inside a specific 1C installation (run a query, call a method, execute a test). What it lacked was the ability to \u003cem\u003efind by meaning\u003c/em\u003e the thing to act on. That is retrieval — the \u003cstrong\u003e\"R\" in RAG\u003c/strong\u003e — and it is what this subsystem adds. Generation stays with the client model; we only retrieve and ground.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eConcretely, it closes gaps like: an AI writing a Vanessa BDD test needs the \u003cstrong\u003ecatalog of available steps\u003c/strong\u003e and the \u003cstrong\u003eexisting scenarios\u003c/strong\u003e so it reuses real, runnable phrases instead of hallucinating step text; or finding a product/client \u003cstrong\u003eby intent\u003c/strong\u003e (\"that construction contractor from Kyiv\", \"red winter jacket\") rather than by exact code.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eDesign decisions (and why)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-design-decisions-and-why\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Design decisions (and why)\" href=\"#design-decisions-and-why\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe shape of the subsystem follows directly from its constraints — they are part of the problem, not incidental:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDecision\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eRationale\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eContent-agnostic core; domain logic lives in outside adapters\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe core never knows about Gherkin, SKUs, or INNs. Adapters (in 1C/BSL) parse the domain and feed the core generic records. One engine serves QA, products, clients, docs — instead of N bespoke searches.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIn-process, in-memory, no disk persistence\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eData is corporate and session-bound (Vanessa runs in the client session); the index lives inside the 1C process and dies with it. A stale cache is worse than none for grounding, so the index is rebuilt from the live source on load.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFlat brute-force vector index (no ANN/Qdrant)\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eTarget scale is ~5–10k records per session. Search is microseconds and RAM is tens of MB — an ANN index would be pure complexity.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHybrid retrieval (dense + keyword), not pure semantic\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eHalf the queries are exact identifiers (SKU, INN, step syntax) where embeddings fail; half are intent where exact match fails. Both channels are needed; keyword is a full scan (cheap at this scale).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAsynchronous ingest (push from 1C)\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEmbedding 5–10k records takes 15–60 s and must never freeze the thin-client UI. Ingest calls return immediately; a background worker embeds off-thread. Progress is observed by polling \u003ccode\u003estats\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAsync-only config/status methods\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e1C forbids synchronous AddIn property access in async-only infobases, so configuration and status go through the async \u003ccode\u003eApplyConfig\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003eGetStatus\u003c/code\u003e methods (see \u003ca href=\"#native-component-api\"\u003eNative Component API\u003c/a\u003e).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003etext\u003c/code\u003e vs. \u003ccode\u003eembed_text\u003c/code\u003e split\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe single biggest quality lever — the adapter decides what gets embedded (an enriched composite) separately from what is stored/returned verbatim, without polluting the core with domain fields.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eMCP tools\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-mcp-tools\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: MCP tools\" href=\"#mcp-tools\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFour search tools are served \u003cstrong\u003enatively by the component\u003c/strong\u003e — they are handled inside the DLL and are \u003cem\u003enot\u003c/em\u003e forwarded to 1C via \u003ccode\u003eExternalEvent\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eTool\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003ePurpose\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSemantic / keyword / hybrid ranked search over indexed segments. Returns hits with \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ecollection\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003escore\u003c/code\u003e, segment line ranges, (optionally) text, and the hit's \u003cstrong\u003eeffective \u003ccode\u003emeta\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — document meta overlaid by segment meta (segment wins on collision), the same view the meta filters match against.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRE2 regex search (linear time, no backreferences/lookaround) over the stored document text. Works the instant a document is ingested — no vectors needed.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eO(1) line-range slice of a raw document by \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e via an offset table. Out-of-range requests are clamped and the actual range is returned.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe collection registry — AI-facing discovery of what is searchable before querying (see \u003ca href=\"#collections-and-the-collection-registry\"\u003eCollections\u003c/a\u003e). Takes no arguments.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e supports three retrieval modes via the \u003ccode\u003emode\u003c/code\u003e argument:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003edense\u003c/strong\u003e (default) — cosine/dot-product similarity over normalized embedding vectors.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ekeyword\u003c/strong\u003e — lexical scoring, no vectors required.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ehybrid\u003c/strong\u003e — Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) of the dense and keyword result lists.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eBoth \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e accept metadata filters (\u003ccode\u003eall\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003eany\u003c/code\u003e clauses) and a \u003ccode\u003ecollection\u003c/code\u003e to scope the query.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eIngest\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-ingest\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Ingest\" href=\"#ingest\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDocuments are pushed into the core through \u003ccode\u003ercore\u003c/code\u003e's JSON ABI. Ingest is \u003cstrong\u003easynchronous\u003c/strong\u003e: the call is accepted under a short lock and returns immediately, while a background worker embeds the segments off-thread. Text-based tools (\u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e) work right after acceptance; dense \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e becomes available once embedding finishes (\u003ccode\u003estats\u003c/code\u003e exposes per-collection progress).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eindex_segments\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — ingest a document as a list of pre-chunked segments (each with optional \u003ccode\u003eembed_text\u003c/code\u003e, line range, and per-segment \u003ccode\u003emeta\u003c/code\u003e). \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e is required so segments can be upserted/deleted.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eindex_raw\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — ingest a raw multi-line document; the core normalizes line endings, builds a line-offset table, stores the full text, and chunks it (line-snapped, by token budget, with overlap). \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e is optional (auto-assigned and returned in the ack). Accepts the same optional \u003ccode\u003ecollection_description\u003c/code\u003e as \u003ccode\u003eindex_segments\u003c/code\u003e (last non-empty wins).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEach segment carries two distinct texts:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003etext\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — stored and returned verbatim (the canonical record).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eembed_text\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e — the enriched composite that actually goes into the vector (name + tags + steps for a scenario; name + brand + category for a product). If omitted, \u003ccode\u003etext\u003c/code\u003e is embedded. This is where the adapter spends its quality budget.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eCollections and the collection registry\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-collections-and-the-collection-registry\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Collections and the collection registry\" href=\"#collections-and-the-collection-registry\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEvery document belongs to a \u003cstrong\u003ecollection\u003c/strong\u003e (a named bucket: \u003ccode\u003eqa_steps\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eproducts\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eclients\u003c/code\u003e, …). Collections are created implicitly on first ingest and can carry a free-text \u003cstrong\u003edescription\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e returns the registry: for each collection its \u003ccode\u003ename\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003edescription\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003en_docs\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003en_segments\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003evector_status\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eempty\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ebuilding\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eready\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eerror\u003c/code\u003e), and \u003ccode\u003etext_ready\u003c/code\u003e. This is how an AI client \u003cstrong\u003ediscovers what is searchable\u003c/strong\u003e before querying — it is exposed both as a \u003ccode\u003eRagDispatch\u003c/code\u003e method and as the fourth \u003cstrong\u003enative MCP tool\u003c/strong\u003e (no arguments):\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"{ \u0026quot;collections\u0026quot;: [\n  { \u0026quot;name\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;qa_steps\u0026quot;, \u0026quot;description\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;Vanessa BDD step catalog\u0026quot;,\n    \u0026quot;n_docs\u0026quot;: 2, \u0026quot;n_segments\u0026quot;: 780, \u0026quot;text_ready\u0026quot;: true, \u0026quot;vector_status\u0026quot;: \u0026quot;ready\u0026quot; }\n] }\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e{ \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"collections\"\u003c/span\u003e: [\n  { \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"name\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003eqa_steps\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"description\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003eVanessa BDD step catalog\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e,\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"n_docs\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e2\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"n_segments\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e780\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"text_ready\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003etrue\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-ent\"\u003e\"vector_status\"\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003eready\u003cspan class=\"pl-pds\"\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e }\n] }\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e scopes by \u003ccode\u003ecollection\u003c/code\u003e: omit it to search \u003cstrong\u003eall\u003c/strong\u003e collections, pass one name, or pass a \u003cstrong\u003ecomma-separated list\u003c/strong\u003e (or a \u003ccode\u003ecollections\u003c/code\u003e array) to search a chosen subset. So an agent can read the registry, pick the relevant collections by description, and search just those.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eEmbedding worker pool\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-embedding-worker-pool\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Embedding worker pool\" href=\"#embedding-worker-pool\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe embedder runs on a background pool whose size is set by \u003ccode\u003eembed_workers\u003c/code\u003e in \u003ccode\u003econfigure\u003c/code\u003e (settable from the 1C form). One worker (default) keeps a single ONNX session and serializes embedding; multiple workers run several sessions concurrently. On CPU this trades memory for throughput (roughly memory-bandwidth bound, not linear in workers); multi-worker forces CPU because concurrent DirectML sessions are not supported. Query embeddings are prioritized so a bulk reindex does not starve live search latency.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eCore method contract (\u003ccode\u003eRagDispatch\u003c/code\u003e)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-core-method-contract-ragdispatch\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Core method contract (RagDispatch)\" href=\"#core-method-contract-ragdispatch\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAll of the following are invoked from 1C via the async \u003ccode\u003eRagDispatch(method, payload)\u003c/code\u003e method (the C++ component forwards the JSON envelope to \u003ccode\u003ercore\u003c/code\u003e verbatim and does not interpret the method name). Each returns \u003ccode\u003e{\"ok\":true,\"result\":…}\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e{\"ok\":false,\"error\":{\"code\",\"message\"}}\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eMethod\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003ePurpose\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003econfigure\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eLoad the model once — \u003ccode\u003emodel\u003c/code\u003e (a built-in name from the whitelist: \u003ccode\u003emultilingual-e5-small\u003c/code\u003e (default) / \u003ccode\u003emultilingual-e5-base\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003emultilingual-e5-large\u003c/code\u003e; an unknown name fails with a structural \u003ccode\u003ebad_model\u003c/code\u003e error listing the supported names), \u003ccode\u003ecache_dir\u003c/code\u003e (directory the built-in model is downloaded into / cached in), \u003ccode\u003emodel_path\u003c/code\u003e (offline local files, bypasses the whitelist), \u003ccode\u003edevice\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003enormalize\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003emax_seq_len\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eintra_threads\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eembed_workers\u003c/code\u003e. \u003ccode\u003edim\u003c/code\u003e is fixed by the model. Idempotent; re-configuring with a different model/dim after indexing implies \u003ccode\u003ereset\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eindex_segments\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAsync ingest of pre-chunked segments (with \u003ccode\u003eembed_text\u003c/code\u003e, line ranges, per-segment \u003ccode\u003emeta\u003c/code\u003e, optional collection \u003ccode\u003edescription\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eindex_raw\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAsync ingest of a raw document; the core chunks it (line-snapped, token-budgeted, overlapping) and builds the offset table. Same optional \u003ccode\u003ecollection_description\u003c/code\u003e as \u003ccode\u003eindex_segments\u003c/code\u003e (last non-empty wins).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRanked retrieval — \u003ccode\u003emode\u003c/code\u003e dense/keyword/hybrid, \u003ccode\u003ecollection\u003c/code\u003e(s), meta filters, \u003ccode\u003ek\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003emin_score\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003emax_per_doc\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003einclude_text\u003c/code\u003e. Hits echo the \u003cstrong\u003eeffective\u003c/strong\u003e meta (doc overlaid by segment, segment wins) — the same view the filters match.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eO(1) line-range slice of a raw document by \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRE2 regex / substring scan over stored text.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003estats\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eGlobal + per-collection counters, model, dim, memory estimate, collection statuses.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe collection registry (names + descriptions + counts + status).\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003elist_models\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThe built-in model whitelist — \u003ccode\u003e{default, models:[…]}\u003c/code\u003e. Works before \u003ccode\u003econfigure\u003c/code\u003e and in the lite/mock build, so a client can always render a model picker.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003edelete_document\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003edelete_collection\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRemove a document (by \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e) or a whole collection.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ereset\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eClear the entire index.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWorking with the subsystem — for the 1C developer\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-working-with-the-subsystem--for-the-1c-developer\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Working with the subsystem — for the 1C developer\" href=\"#working-with-the-subsystem--for-the-1c-developer\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eYou write \u003cstrong\u003eadapters\u003c/strong\u003e: thin BSL that turns a domain object into generic records and pushes them in. The core stays domain-free.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAttach + configure once.\u003c/strong\u003e Attach the full component, then \u003ccode\u003econfigure\u003c/code\u003e via \u003ccode\u003eRagDispatch\u003c/code\u003e with a built-in \u003ccode\u003emodel\u003c/code\u003e name (or a local \u003ccode\u003emodel_path\u003c/code\u003e, plus optionally \u003ccode\u003ecache_dir\u003c/code\u003e) and \u003ccode\u003eembed_workers\u003c/code\u003e. (Component-level settings — logging/timeout — go through \u003ccode\u003eApplyConfig\u003c/code\u003e, never through synchronous properties.)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eIndex asynchronously.\u003c/strong\u003e For each domain object build segments — set \u003ccode\u003etext\u003c/code\u003e (verbatim) and \u003ccode\u003eembed_text\u003c/code\u003e (the enriched composite), attach \u003ccode\u003emeta\u003c/code\u003e (\u003ccode\u003e{sku, inn, type, tags, …}\u003c/code\u003e) and a \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e for upsert — and call \u003ccode\u003eindex_segments\u003c/code\u003e. The call returns immediately; embedding happens in the background.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWatch progress\u003c/strong\u003e by polling \u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003estats\u003c/code\u003e until \u003ccode\u003evector_status = ready\u003c/code\u003e. Text tools (\u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e) work the instant a document is accepted; dense \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e lights up when embedding finishes.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eKeep it fresh within the session.\u003c/strong\u003e When one object changes, re-\u003ccode\u003eindex_segments\u003c/code\u003e just that \u003ccode\u003edoc_id\u003c/code\u003e (atomic upsert) or \u003ccode\u003edelete_document\u003c/code\u003e — never rebuild the whole corpus.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAll file/content reads stay client-side\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003e\u0026amp;AtClient\u003c/code\u003e) in the reference form, so source files are read from the operator's machine, not the server.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe reference end-to-end flow is exercised headlessly by the RAG self-test (stub QA/products/clients adapters → index → embed → search, asserting pass/fail). See the \u003ccode\u003eonec-rag-selftest\u003c/code\u003e skill.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eWorking with the subsystem — for the AI agent / MCP client\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-working-with-the-subsystem--for-the-ai-agent--mcp-client\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Working with the subsystem — for the AI agent / MCP client\" href=\"#working-with-the-subsystem--for-the-ai-agent--mcp-client\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFrom the client side it is just four MCP tools, but used in a deliberate order:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDiscover.\u003c/strong\u003e Call \u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e first to see what this specific 1C base has indexed and read each collection's description — don't assume; the corpus is per-installation and per-session.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSearch by meaning, scoped.\u003c/strong\u003e Call \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e with \u003ccode\u003emode: hybrid\u003c/code\u003e for anything involving exact identifiers (SKU/INN/article/step syntax) and \u003ccode\u003edense\u003c/code\u003e for pure intent. Scope to the relevant \u003ccode\u003ecollection\u003c/code\u003e(s) from step 1, or omit to search everything. Use meta filters (\u003ccode\u003eall\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003eany\u003c/code\u003e) to narrow.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGround, don't hallucinate.\u003c/strong\u003e Treat hits as the source of truth for \u003cem\u003ethis\u003c/em\u003e base. For QA, prefer canonical step phrases and existing scenarios returned by search over inventing them; a reverse step→scenario lookup (a QA-adapter convenience over the same \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e) surfaces real usages with real parameters.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eZoom in.\u003c/strong\u003e Use \u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e to pull the exact line range of a hit verbatim, and \u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e for exact-string / regex confirmation (it works even before vectors are ready). A \u003ccode\u003ebuilding\u003c/code\u003e collection returns partial results flagged as incomplete — retry once it is \u003ccode\u003eready\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe contract is: \u003cstrong\u003ethe client retrieves and grounds on what actually exists in this base right now; it never swallows the whole corpus into context and never relies on exact match alone.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eLite vs full\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-lite-vs-full\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Lite vs full\" href=\"#lite-vs-full\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe same \u003ccode\u003elibhttp1cWin.dll\u003c/code\u003e ships in \u003cstrong\u003etwo distributions\u003c/strong\u003e. The component is pure C++ built \u003ccode\u003e/MT\u003c/code\u003e and does \u003cstrong\u003enot\u003c/strong\u003e link the Rust core; instead it loads an optional \u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e at runtime (\u003ccode\u003eLoadLibrary\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003eGetProcAddress\u003c/code\u003e, resolved next to the component DLL — see \u003ccode\u003ehttp-1c-dll/src/RustCore.h\u003c/code\u003e):\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eDistribution\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eContents\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eSearch behavior\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003elite\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003elibhttp1cWin.dll\u003c/code\u003e only\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e are advertised in \u003ccode\u003etools/list\u003c/code\u003e but return a structured error (\u003ccode\u003ecode: rag_not_installed\u003c/code\u003e) telling the caller to install the RAG package.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003efull\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003elibhttp1cWin.dll\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e + \u003ccode\u003eDirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eReal fastembed/onnxruntime search core; the four tools run for real.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDetection is automatic at runtime: if \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e is present next to the component \u003cstrong\u003eand\u003c/strong\u003e all four ABI entry points (\u003ccode\u003ercore_version\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ercore_dispatch\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ercore_free_string\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003ercore_shutdown\u003c/code\u003e) resolve, the component runs the full search path. A missing, partial, or version-mismatched \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e degrades cleanly to the lite path (an \"install RAG\" error) — never a crash. The tool schemas are identical in both distributions, so a client sees the same \u003ccode\u003etools/list\u003c/code\u003e either way.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eGPU / CPU (DirectML)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-gpu--cpu-directml\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: GPU / CPU (DirectML)\" href=\"#gpu--cpu-directml\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe full search core uses onnxruntime's \u003cstrong\u003eDirectML\u003c/strong\u003e execution provider for GPU acceleration, with \u003cstrong\u003eautomatic CPU fallback\u003c/strong\u003e when no compatible GPU is available. The device is selected via the \u003ccode\u003econfigure\u003c/code\u003e method's \u003ccode\u003edevice\u003c/code\u003e field:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003e\u003ccode\u003edevice\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eBehavior\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eauto\u003c/code\u003e (default)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDirectML with onnxruntime's automatic CPU fallback.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003edml\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eForce the DirectML (GPU) execution provider.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecpu\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eForce CPU execution.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e\u003ccode\u003eDirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e is a hard dependency of the full package.\u003c/strong\u003e ort's prebuilt onnxruntime bundles the DirectML provider, so \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e hard-imports \u003ccode\u003eDirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e. The \u003cstrong\u003efull\u003c/strong\u003e bundle must ship \u003ccode\u003eDirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e alongside \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e, otherwise \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e fails to load and the component silently falls back to the lite \"install RAG\" behavior. On Windows it lives at \u003ccode\u003eC:\\Windows\\System32\\DirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e. (Version-coupling caveat: this \u003ccode\u003eDirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e is paired with the bundled onnxruntime; a future CPU-only onnxruntime build would remove the import and this DLL could be dropped.)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe embedding model itself is \u003cstrong\u003efetched at runtime\u003c/strong\u003e, not at build time — nothing model-related is downloaded during the build or in CI.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eVanessa Automation Integration\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-vanessa-automation-integration\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Vanessa Automation Integration\" href=\"#vanessa-automation-integration\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe repository ships a ready-made integration for \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/Pr-Mex/vanessa-automation\"\u003eVanessa Automation\u003c/a\u003e (1C BDD testing): the \u003cstrong\u003eMCPRagSearch\u003c/strong\u003e plugin in \u003ca href=\"/DitriXNew/MCP-DB-Client/blob/master/vanessa-plugin/README.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003evanessa-plugin/\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e. It runs the http1c component inside the VA session and serves Vanessa's \u003cstrong\u003eentire MCP contract\u003c/strong\u003e through it — all VA tools are collected from MCPVA via a small facade (a micro-hook already present in the VA fork), while \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003eget_segment\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e run natively over the indexed VA step library (\u003ccode\u003evanessa_steps\u003c/code\u003e), the VA knowledge base (\u003ccode\u003evanessa_kb\u003c/code\u003e), and arbitrary file collections. See \u003ca href=\"/DitriXNew/MCP-DB-Client/blob/master/vanessa-plugin/README.md\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003evanessa-plugin/README.md\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/a\u003e (Russian) for setup, the collections, the \u003ccode\u003esearch-guide\u003c/code\u003e prompt / \u003ccode\u003eget_search_guide\u003c/code\u003e tool, and the auto-reindex watcher.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eRepository Layout\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-repository-layout\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Repository Layout\" href=\"#repository-layout\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" pure C++, no Rust/cargo involved. To also build the \u003cstrong\u003efull\u003c/strong\u003e search core (\u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e), configure CMake with \u003ccode\u003e-DRCORE_FASTEMBED=ON\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"cd http-1c-dll \u0026amp;\u0026amp; mkdir -p build \u0026amp;\u0026amp; cd build\n# lite — libhttp1cWin.dll alone (no cargo):\ncmake .. -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release\ncmake --build .\n\n# full — also builds rcore.dll via cargo:\ncmake .. -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DRCORE_FASTEMBED=ON\ncmake --build .\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ecd\u003c/span\u003e http-1c-dll \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026amp;\u0026amp;\u003c/span\u003e mkdir -p build \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e\u0026amp;\u0026amp;\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ecd\u003c/span\u003e build\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e lite — libhttp1cWin.dll alone (no cargo):\u003c/span\u003e\ncmake .. -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release\ncmake --build \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e full — also builds rcore.dll via cargo:\u003c/span\u003e\ncmake .. -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DRCORE_FASTEMBED=ON\ncmake --build \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe full build invokes \u003ccode\u003ecargo build --release --features fastembed\u003c/code\u003e (handled inside CMake) and drops \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e next to the component in \u003ccode\u003ehttp-1c-dll/bin/\u003c/code\u003e. The embedding model is fetched at runtime, so no model is downloaded at build time.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhy two binaries (\u003ccode\u003e/MT\u003c/code\u003e vs \u003ccode\u003e/MD\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/strong\u003e The C++ component is built with the \u003cstrong\u003estatic\u003c/strong\u003e CRT (\u003ccode\u003e/MT\u003c/code\u003e) — it cannot compile under \u003ccode\u003e/MD\u003c/code\u003e because MSVC's \u003ccode\u003echar16_t\u003c/code\u003e stream code hits error C2491. onnxruntime (via \u003ccode\u003eort\u003c/code\u003e), however, requires the \u003cstrong\u003edynamic\u003c/strong\u003e CRT (\u003ccode\u003e/MD\u003c/code\u003e). Linking the two together is impossible, so the search core is a \u003cem\u003eseparate\u003c/em\u003e \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e (a cdylib) that the \u003ccode\u003e/MT\u003c/code\u003e component loads at runtime instead of linking. CMake builds the cdylib with the dynamic CRT by removing the \u003ccode\u003e+crt-static\u003c/code\u003e default (\u003ccode\u003eRUSTFLAGS=-C target-feature=-crt-static\u003c/code\u003e); this is independent of the component's \u003ccode\u003e/MT\u003c/code\u003e and they never link together.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003ePackaging the add-in (lite / full)\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-packaging-the-add-in-lite--full\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Packaging the add-in (lite / full)\" href=\"#packaging-the-add-in-lite--full\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ebuild/package-http1c-addin.sh\u003c/code\u003e assembles a 1C add-in ZIP (and updates \u003ccode\u003eTemplate.bin\u003c/code\u003e). The variant is selected via the \u003ccode\u003eVARIANT\u003c/code\u003e env var:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"# lite (default) — libhttp1cWin.dll only:\nVARIANT=lite bash build/package-http1c-addin.sh\n\n# full — also bundles rcore.dll + DirectML.dll:\nVARIANT=full bash build/package-http1c-addin.sh\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e lite (default) — libhttp1cWin.dll only:\u003c/span\u003e\nVARIANT=lite bash build/package-http1c-addin.sh\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e full — also bundles rcore.dll + DirectML.dll:\u003c/span\u003e\nVARIANT=full bash build/package-http1c-addin.sh\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003cstrong\u003efull\u003c/strong\u003e packaging step copies \u003ccode\u003eDirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e from \u003ccode\u003eC:\\Windows\\System32\\DirectML.dll\u003c/code\u003e (override with \u003ccode\u003eDIRECTML_SRC\u003c/code\u003e) into the bundle — it is a hard dependency of \u003ccode\u003ercore.dll\u003c/code\u003e (see \u003ca href=\"#gpu--cpu-directml\"\u003eGPU / CPU\u003c/a\u003e) and the script fails clearly if it is missing. Set \u003ccode\u003eSKIP_TEMPLATE=1\u003c/code\u003e to produce only the ZIP without overwriting the committed \u003ccode\u003eTemplate.bin\u003c/code\u003e (which tracks the lite bundle).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe release workflow (\u003ccode\u003e.github/workflows/release.yml\u003c/code\u003e) builds both variants and attaches both ZIPs to the GitHub dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eWindows-focused setup. The build scripts target MSVC and NMake via bash.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe build still depends on Windows toolchain availability through \u003ccode\u003ePATH\u003c/code\u003e or an initialized Visual Studio build environment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe HTTP server is intentionally local-only and binds to \u003ccode\u003e127.0.0.1\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eexecute\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eevaluate\u003c/code\u003e expose powerful server-side capabilities and should only be used in trusted local environments.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThe current tool set mixes practical helpers with demo functionality such as \u003ccode\u003erunLongTask\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eThere is no packaging or deployment flow yet for distributing the add-in as a polished product.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eTesting \u0026amp; Deployment Notes\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-testing--deployment-notes\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Testing \u0026amp; Deployment Notes\" href=\"#testing--deployment-notes\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen updating the native add-in DLL during development:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eClear the component cache.\u003c/strong\u003e 1C caches native add-ins in a temporary directory. Delete the cache folder before loading a new version:\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"%APPDATA%\\1C\\1cv8\\ExtCompT\"\u003e\u003cpre lang=\"text\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e%APPDATA%\\1C\\1cv8\\ExtCompT\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRestart the 1C session.\u003c/strong\u003e Close the 1C application completely and reopen it — a running session keeps the old DLL locked.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDisable dangerous action protection.\u003c/strong\u003e In the 1C user settings, uncheck \u003cem\u003e\"Protection from dangerous actions\"\u003c/em\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eЗащита от опасных действий\u003c/code\u003e). Otherwise the platform will block add-in attachment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWithout these steps the platform may silently load an outdated DLL or refuse to attach the add-in.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eVersion\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-version\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Version\" href=\"#version\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe native component version defined in the source is:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"1.5.0\"\u003e\u003cpre lang=\"text\" class=\"notranslate\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003e1.5.0\n\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRecent changes:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e1.5.0\u003c/strong\u003e — Async-only config/status. Added the async \u003ccode\u003eApplyConfig\u003c/code\u003e and \u003ccode\u003eGetStatus\u003c/code\u003e methods so the component is fully usable from async-only infobases (where synchronous AddIn property access throws \u003cem\u003e\"Cannot call synchronous methods on the client\"\u003c/em\u003e). The reference form no longer touches a property on the client. Search subsystem gained the \u003cstrong\u003ecollection registry\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003elist_collections\u003c/code\u003e, per-collection descriptions, multi-collection \u003ccode\u003esearch\u003c/code\u003e) and a configurable \u003cstrong\u003eembedding worker pool\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003eembed_workers\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eLicense\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-license-1\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: License\" href=\"#license-1\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis project is licensed under the MIT License. (#2)

* Add Rust search core: Stage 0 FFI skeleton + Stage 1 core slice

rust-core/ (crate rcore): C ABI (JSON in/out, rust_free_string, +crt-static),
process-singleton store, Embedder trait + deterministic MockEmbedder, async
index_segments worker with two-axis text_ready/vector_status state machine,
configure/search(dense)/stats/reset dispatch, rcore_shutdown joins the worker.

http-1c-dll: link rcore staticlib into the DLL via CMake; RustCore.h RAII wrapper.
CI/release workflows: set up the Rust toolchain before the DLL build.
.devtool/: kanban board tracking stages 0-5 (Stage 0+1 cards done).

Verified locally: cargo test 27/27; full Release DLL build links rcore.lib into
libhttp1cWin.dll (/MT <-> +crt-static match holds). Real fastembed/ort embedder
and native tools/call routing are deferred to follow-up cards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Stage 2: sync text install on accept, meta filters, grep

Task 1 (carried-forward fix): install a doc's text segments into the store
synchronously at accept (vector=None, text_ready=true, vector_status=Building);
the background worker now fills ONLY the vectors afterward, matched by
(doc_id, segment_id). Re-ingest stays atomic — a superseded job's stale segment
ids simply don't match and are skipped. Net: grep/keyword/get_segment see text
the instant accept returns; only dense search waits for vectors.

Task 2: new filter.rs with combinable, domain-agnostic meta filters — all (AND),
any (OR), tags_all, tags_any — evaluated over a hit's effective meta (document
meta merged with segment meta; segment overrides doc on key collision). Wired
into both search and grep.

Task 3: new grep.rs regex search over stored segment text using regex 1.12.3
(RE2, linear-time, no ReDoS) via RegexBuilder (case_insensitive, multi_line).
CRLF->LF normalized; 1-based inclusive line numbers per segment; context_lines,
max_matches (sets truncated) and max_per_doc honored. A broken pattern returns a
structural bad_pattern error envelope, never a panic.
