* Stage 2: index_raw chunker + offset table + get_segment

Add two coupled dispatch methods to the Rust search core (rcore):

index_raw (async, like index_segments): on accept under the short write
lock it normalizes the text CRLF->LF, builds a full-document line offset
table, stores the full normalized text + offset table on the doc, chunks
the text into line-snapped segments (vector=None), and enqueues one embed
job. Returns {accepted, collection, doc_id, segment_count} immediately;
the worker fills only vectors off-thread. doc_id is auto-assigned and
returned in the ack when omitted; provided ids upsert atomically.

Chunker: greedy line-granular split by token budget (target ~300, hard
cap = max_seq_len) snapped to line boundaries with ~2-line overlap; a
single line over the cap becomes one oversized chunk (truncated for embed
only, returned whole by get_segment). Token counting is a documented
heuristic (max of whitespace-word count and chars/4) in one function, to
be swapped for the real tokenizer when the real embedder lands.

get_segment: O(1) line-range slice over the offset table; out-of-range
clamps and returns the actual {line_start, line_end}; respects max_lines;
works the instant after accept (text+offsets are synchronous). Atomic
index_segments records (no offset table) -> structural no_line_index
error; unknown doc_id -> not_found.

Extend Document with optional full_text + line_offsets (raw docs only);
index_segments behavior unchanged. Add NOT_FOUND / NO_LINE_INDEX codes.

Tests: chunk budget/line-snap/overlap, oversized single line, offset
table, get_segment slice/clamp/max_lines/no_line_index, CRLF norm,
get_segment immediately after accept, auto doc_id in ack, raw doc
greppable immediately and dense-searchable when ready. Also drain the
shared background worker in the test harness guards to remove a latent
cross-test race (a stale job flipping a same-named collection Ready
early); test-only, no production change. 67 tests pass.

* Stage 2: hybrid (RRF) search modes + incremental delete

Hybrid search (mode: dense | keyword | hybrid, default dense):
- keyword: full lexical scan over stored segment text; scores by
  distinct-query-term coverage + 0.1*occurrences (whole-token match).
  Works while vector_status==Building (no vectors needed).
- hybrid: fuses dense + keyword rank lists with Reciprocal Rank Fusion
  (k=60) keyed by segment_id, so a doc strong in only one channel still
  surfaces (the SKU/ИНН case).
- min_score is applied per the active mode and documented as such:
  dense=absolute cosine floor, keyword=match-score floor, hybrid=fused
  RRF-score floor (relative, not a cosine).
- All modes respect collection, k, max_per_doc, include_text, meta
  filters; dense/hybrid keep the partial flag for Building collections,
  keyword is never partial.

Incremental delete (atomic, under one short write lock):
- delete_document(doc_id): removes the doc and all its segments; drops
  the collection if it becomes empty; unknown doc_id -> {deleted:false}.
- delete_collection(collection): removes the whole collection; unknown
  -> {deleted:false}. In-flight embeds for deleted docs/collections
  become harmless no-ops via the existing stale-id / missing-collection
  guards in apply_job.

16 new tests (keyword ranking, hybrid RRF fusion both channels,
keyword/hybrid honor filter+k+max_per_doc, keyword while Building,
delete removes from search+grep+get_segment, delete_collection clears,
deletes of unknown -> structural false). 83 tests pass (67 prior + 16).

* Load rcore.dll at runtime for lite/full packaging

The 1C C++ component is /MT and cannot compile under /MD (char16_t
streams hit MSVC C2491), but ort/onnxruntime requires /MD. So the real
search core ships as a separate rcore.dll (cdylib, /MD, static
onnxruntime) that the component loads at RUNTIME via LoadLibrary +
GetProcAddress instead of link-time staticlib coupling. One
libhttp1cWin.dll now serves both packages:
  * lite  — rcore.dll absent  -> search tools return "install RAG"
  * full  — rcore.dll present -> real fastembed search

- rust-core/Cargo.toml: crate-type staticlib+cdylib -> cdylib only;
  the component no longer links a staticlib.
- RustCore.h: replace the extern "C" link-time decls with a lazy,
  thread-safe (std::call_once) loader (RCore) that locates rcore.dll
  NEXT TO this module (GetModuleHandleExW FROM_ADDRESS +
  GetModuleFileNameW, not cwd), resolves all 4 entry points, exposes
  available()/dispatch()/shutdown()/version(), and frees via the loaded
  rcore_free_string pointer. Robust no-op when rcore.dll is absent.
- HttpServerComponent.cpp: dispatchNativeTool returns a structured
  rag_not_installed result when !RCore::available(); doStopListen calls
  RCore::shutdown(). tools/list surface stays uniform across lite/full.
- CMakeLists.txt: drop the rcore staticlib link (lite is pure C++/MT,
  no cargo dependency); add option(RCORE_FASTEMBED) that builds the
  cdylib with the dynamic CRT and copies rcore.dll into bin/ for the
  full package.

* rust-core search: lightweight Scored ranking, top-k select, SIMD dot

Rewrite the search ranking path to defer Hit materialization to the ≤k
survivors instead of building (and deep-cloning doc.meta + segment text)
a full Hit for every scanned segment:

- New Scored<'a> carries borrows during scoring/fusion — zero per-segment
  allocation; dense/keyword channels return unsorted Scored.
- finalize() does a single bounded top-k via select_nth_unstable_by (no
  full sort when max_per_doc is None), then materializes Hits for survivors.
- make_hit honors include_text: clones only the PREVIEW_CHARS prefix when
  the caller doesn't want full text (was always cloning the whole segment).
- rrf_fuse operates on lightweight refs.
- dot(): 8-lane chunked accumulator LLVM can auto-vectorize, replacing the
  serial zip().sum() dependency chain (closes the SIMD bullet of perf-ann).
- apply_job moves the embedding out of the job (mem::take) instead of
  cloning it under the write lock.

Closes perf-topk-heap-deferred-hit. 85/85 cargo test green (adds top-k
ordering + include_text:false preview-cap tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rust-core: cache keyword token multiset; borrow LF-only text in grep

- Each Segment now caches a token multiset (kw_counts) built once at index
  time from its text, with the same tokenizer the query uses. keyword_score
  is now O(query_terms) hash lookups with no per-query re-tokenization or
  Vec<String> allocation. Populated in both ingest paths (accept_index,
  index_raw chunker).
- grep's normalize_newlines returns Cow: a single \r scan lets LF-only text
  (every index_raw segment, plus any LF source) be borrowed with zero
  allocation instead of two String-allocating replace passes.

The deeper zero-copy grep writer (borrowing &str in GrepHit, hoisting
per-doc fields) is left as a documented follow-up: GrepHit owns its strings
and clones per match (bounded by max_matches), and borrowing would put a
lifetime on the public result type.

Closes perf-grep-keyword-cache-tokens. 86/86 cargo test green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cpp glue: fix WCHAR2MB/MB2WCHAR data race (thread-safe Win32 convert)

WCHAR2MB and MB2WCHAR used a process-wide `static std::wstring_convert`,
whose mutable internal state is not thread-safe. httplib runs a worker pool
and the MCP path transcodes UTF-16<->UTF-8 on every request/response, so
concurrent calls raced -> corrupted UTF-8 / sporadic crashes under load.

- Windows: convert via WideCharToMultiByte / MultiByteToWideChar directly,
  with locals only (no shared state). MB2WCHAR now does one allocation
  instead of the previous wstring->u16string double copy.
- Non-Windows: the wstring_convert is now a local (was static), so each
  call owns its converter — also race-free.

Signatures unchanged, so all 24 call sites are untouched. Syntax-checked
with MSVC (cl /Zs /std:c++17 /utf-8 + project defines); full compile in CI.

Closes perf-wstring-convert-race (the critical race). Converting straight
into the 1C manager buffer + a concurrent-load test are documented as a
deferred micro-optimization. Also records the deliberate deferral of
perf-ffi-typed-serde (low ROI / high wire-contract risk).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rust-core embedder: tune ONNX sessions per role (query CPU, bulk device)

The two model instances split reindex vs query by lock but not by CPU: each
session defaulted to all-cores intra-op, so reindex and queries oversubscribed
and fought over cores. Now derive per-role SessionTuning from device +
config.intra_threads:

- query: CPU execution provider + min(2, ncpu) intra-op threads. A single
  short query is latency-bound and DirectML loses to CPU there (host<->device
  copies); a small pool also avoids spin-up cost.
- bulk: the configured device (GPU/DirectML when asked) + the explicit

* rust-core: configurable multi-session embedding worker pool (+ 1C control)

Adds an opt-in concurrent bulk-embedding pool, settable from 1C via the
configure payload's `embed_workers` (and a `workers=` launch param):
absent ⇒ 1 (single worker, unchanged default), 0 ⇒ auto (≈ncpu/2), n ⇒ n.

- core.rs: the ingest worker is now N threads sharing one job queue (shared
  mpsc receiver; the lock is held only for the brief dequeue, so N embeds run
  concurrently). Worker thread count = embedder.bulk_concurrency(). shutdown
  sends one Stop per thread and joins all.
- fastembed_embedder.rs: FastEmbedder holds a pool of bulk sessions; the
  worker round-robins onto them (N consecutive cursor values ⇒ N distinct
  sessions ⇒ true 1:1 parallelism). Two correctness fixes found by the
  feature: (1) multi-worker forces CPU for the bulk pool — N concurrent
  DirectML sessions on one GPU error out, and a GPU already parallelizes
  within one session; (2) the ONNX sub-batch shrinks to ≈256/N so N
  concurrent sessions don't multiply peak memory and OOM. `run` now surfaces
  embed errors (eprintln) instead of silently returning zeros — that swallow
  is what hid the DirectML failure.
- embed.rs: Embedder::bulk_concurrency() (default 1).
- lib.rs: parse + echo embed_workers.
- Module.bsl (build-22): `embedperf=<dir>` benchmark — reads every *.feature,
  chunks by scenario, indexes with real embedding in batches, polls until the
  worker drains, reports total time + throughput; `workers=N` and `batch=N`
  launch params; periodic progress lines. Gated — perf=0/no-embedperf keeps
  the normal contour.

Measured on the 7561-scenario corpus (CPU, MiniLM-L6): 1w 536s/14 seg/s,
3w 425s/17, 6w 381s/19 — ~1.4× max (memory-bandwidth bound, not ~Nx).
0 failed after the memory fix. 86/86 cargo test; fastembed builds clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rust-core: worker-pool perf fixes from pool review

Seven fixes to the multi-session embedding pool:

- Pin each worker thread to its own model session (Embedder::
  embed_passages_at(slot)) instead of a shared round-robin cursor: with
  uneven job durations the cursor could queue a worker behind a busy
  session while other sessions idled (head-of-line blocking). The cursor
  stays as the fallback for callers without a stable slot; `slot % n`
  guards retired threads against a smaller resized pool.
- Resize the worker thread pool when reconfigure changes the embedder's
  bulk_concurrency: previously the pool was spawned once and a later
  `embed_workers` change silently loaded M sessions driven by the old N
  threads. Old threads get one Stop each (their FIFO queue drains first)
  and are stashed in retired_workers; joins happen only in shutdown(),
  never under the CORE write lock (a worker may be blocked in apply_job
  on that lock — joining there would deadlock).
- Split large ingests into EMBED_JOB_CHUNK(=256)-sized embed jobs so one
  huge index_segments/index_raw spreads across the whole pool instead of
  serializing on one worker. pending_jobs bumps by the chunk count; an
  empty doc still enqueues exactly one job so the collection can flip
  Ready (single helper keeps bump+enqueue from drifting).
- Move O(total text) accept work (kw multisets; raw normalize/offsets/
  chunking) out of the global write lock: prepare_index[_raw] runs
  before the lock in lib.rs, commit_index[_raw] does only id allocation
  + install + enqueue under it; accept_* kept as thin wrappers for tests.
- Cap auto worker count (embed_workers=0) at (ncpu/2).clamp(1,4):
  measured scaling saturates ~1.4x (memory-bandwidth bound), so more
  sessions only waste one model copy of RAM each.
- Clamp explicit intra_threads to the per-session fair share (ncpu/m)
  when m > 1, preserving the no-oversubscription invariant.
- Surface the last embed failure via stats (last_embed_error):
  eprintln! is invisible inside the 1C host process; FastEmbedder now
  records the most recent ort/poisoned-lock error behind
  Embedder::last_error().

Tests: +2 (chunked 600-segment ingest converges with pending_jobs==3;
pool resize 2->4 retires/joins cleanly, both collections Ready).
89/89 cargo test; cargo check --features fastembed clean.

Copilot review round 2 on PR #2 - all three confirmed real:

  validateAuth (security-critical race): now guarded by authTokenMutex,

* Stage 2: sync text install on accept, meta filters, grep

Task 1 (carried-forward fix): install a doc's text segments into the store
synchronously at accept (vector=None, text_ready=true, vector_status=Building);
the background worker now fills ONLY the vectors afterward, matched by
(doc_id, segment_id). Re-ingest stays atomic — a superseded job's stale segment
ids simply don't match and are skipped. Net: grep/keyword/get_segment see text
the instant accept returns; only dense search waits for vectors.

Task 2: new filter.rs with combinable, domain-agnostic meta filters — all (AND),
any (OR), tags_all, tags_any — evaluated over a hit's effective meta (document
meta merged with segment meta; segment overrides doc on key collision). Wired
into both search and grep.

(RE2, linear-time, no ReDoS) via RegexBuilder (case_insensitive, multi_line).
CRLF->LF normalized; 1-based inclusive line numbers per segment; context_lines,
max_matches (sets truncated) and max_per_doc honored. A broken pattern returns a
structural bad_pattern error envelope, never a panic.

* Stage 2: index_raw chunker + offset table + get_segment

Add two coupled dispatch methods to the Rust search core (rcore):

index_raw (async, like index_segments): on accept under the short write
lock it normalizes the text CRLF->LF, builds a full-document line offset
table, stores the full normalized text + offset table on the doc, chunks
the text into line-snapped segments (vector=None), and enqueues one embed
job. Returns {accepted, collection, doc_id, segment_count} immediately;
the worker fills only vectors off-thread. doc_id is auto-assigned and
returned in the ack when omitted; provided ids upsert atomically.

Chunker: greedy line-granular split by token budget (target ~300, hard
cap = max_seq_len) snapped to line boundaries with ~2-line overlap; a
single line over the cap becomes one oversized chunk (truncated for embed
only, returned whole by get_segment). Token counting is a documented
heuristic (max of whitespace-word count and chars/4) in one function, to
be swapped for the real tokenizer when the real embedder lands.

get_segment: O(1) line-range slice over the offset table; out-of-range
clamps and returns the actual {line_start, line_end}; respects max_lines;
works the instant after accept (text+offsets are synchronous). Atomic
index_segments records (no offset table) -> structural no_line_index
error; unknown doc_id -> not_found.

Extend Document with optional full_text + line_offsets (raw docs only);
index_segments behavior unchanged. Add NOT_FOUND / NO_LINE_INDEX codes.

Tests: chunk budget/line-snap/overlap, oversized single line, offset
table, get_segment slice/clamp/max_lines/no_line_index, CRLF norm,
get_segment immediately after accept, auto doc_id in ack, raw doc
greppable immediately and dense-searchable when ready. Also drain the
shared background worker in the test harness guards to remove a latent
cross-test race (a stale job flipping a same-named collection Ready
early); test-only, no production change. 67 tests pass.

* Stage 2: hybrid (RRF) search modes + incremental delete

Hybrid search (mode: dense | keyword | hybrid, default dense):
- keyword: full lexical scan over stored segment text; scores by
  distinct-query-term coverage + 0.1*occurrences (whole-token match).
  Works while vector_status==Building (no vectors needed).
- hybrid: fuses dense + keyword rank lists with Reciprocal Rank Fusion
  (k=60) keyed by segment_id, so a doc strong in only one channel still
  surfaces (the SKU/ИНН case).
- min_score is applied per the active mode and documented as such:
  dense=absolute cosine floor, keyword=match-score floor, hybrid=fused
  RRF-score floor (relative, not a cosine).
- All modes respect collection, k, max_per_doc, include_text, meta
  filters; dense/hybrid keep the partial flag for Building collections,
  keyword is never partial.

Incremental delete (atomic, under one short write lock):
- delete_document(doc_id): removes the doc and all its segments; drops
  the collection if it becomes empty; unknown doc_id -> {deleted:false}.
- delete_collection(collection): removes the whole collection; unknown
  -> {deleted:false}. In-flight embeds for deleted docs/collections
  become harmless no-ops via the existing stale-id / missing-collection
  guards in apply_job.

16 new tests (keyword ranking, hybrid RRF fusion both channels,
keyword/hybrid honor filter+k+max_per_doc, keyword while Building,
delete removes from search+grep+get_segment, delete_collection clears,
deletes of unknown -> structural false). 83 tests pass (67 prior + 16).

* Load rcore.dll at runtime for lite/full packaging

The 1C C++ component is /MT and cannot compile under /MD (char16_t
streams hit MSVC C2491), but ort/onnxruntime requires /MD. So the real
search core ships as a separate rcore.dll (cdylib, /MD, static
onnxruntime) that the component loads at RUNTIME via LoadLibrary +
GetProcAddress instead of link-time staticlib coupling. One
libhttp1cWin.dll now serves both packages:
  * lite  — rcore.dll absent  -> search tools return "install RAG"
  * full  — rcore.dll present -> real fastembed search

- rust-core/Cargo.toml: crate-type staticlib+cdylib -> cdylib only;
  the component no longer links a staticlib.
- RustCore.h: replace the extern "C" link-time decls with a lazy,
  thread-safe (std::call_once) loader (RCore) that locates rcore.dll
  NEXT TO this module (GetModuleHandleExW FROM_ADDRESS +
  GetModuleFileNameW, not cwd), resolves all 4 entry points, exposes
  available()/dispatch()/shutdown()/version(), and frees via the loaded
  rcore_free_string pointer. Robust no-op when rcore.dll is absent.
- HttpServerComponent.cpp: dispatchNativeTool returns a structured
  rag_not_installed result when !RCore::available(); doStopListen calls
  RCore::shutdown(). tools/list surface stays uniform across lite/full.
- CMakeLists.txt: drop the rcore staticlib link (lite is pure C++/MT,
  no cargo dependency); add option(RCORE_FASTEMBED) that builds the
  cdylib with the dynamic CRT and copies rcore.dll into bin/ for the
  full package.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rust-core search: lightweight Scored ranking, top-k select, SIMD dot

Rewrite the search ranking path to defer Hit materialization to the ≤k
survivors instead of building (and deep-cloning doc.meta + segment text)
a full Hit for every scanned segment:

- New Scored<'a> carries borrows during scoring/fusion — zero per-segment
  allocation; dense/keyword channels return unsorted Scored.
- finalize() does a single bounded top-k via select_nth_unstable_by (no
  full sort when max_per_doc is None), then materializes Hits for survivors.
- make_hit honors include_text: clones only the PREVIEW_CHARS prefix when
  the caller doesn't want full text (was always cloning the whole segment).
- rrf_fuse operates on lightweight refs.
- dot(): 8-lane chunked accumulator LLVM can auto-vectorize, replacing the
  serial zip().sum() dependency chain (closes the SIMD bullet of perf-ann).
- apply_job moves the embedding out of the job (mem::take) instead of
  cloning it under the write lock.

Closes perf-topk-heap-deferred-hit. 85/85 cargo test green (adds top-k
ordering + include_text:false preview-cap tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rust-core: cache keyword token multiset; borrow LF-only text in grep

- Each Segment now caches a token multiset (kw_counts) built once at index
  time from its text, with the same tokenizer the query uses. keyword_score
  is now O(query_terms) hash lookups with no per-query re-tokenization or
  Vec<String> allocation. Populated in both ingest paths (accept_index,
  index_raw chunker).
- grep's normalize_newlines returns Cow: a single \r scan lets LF-only text
  (every index_raw segment, plus any LF source) be borrowed with zero
  allocation instead of two String-allocating replace passes.

The deeper zero-copy grep writer (borrowing &str in GrepHit, hoisting
per-doc fields) is left as a documented follow-up: GrepHit owns its strings
and clones per match (bounded by max_matches), and borrowing would put a
lifetime on the public result type.

Closes perf-grep-keyword-cache-tokens. 86/86 cargo test green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cpp glue: fix WCHAR2MB/MB2WCHAR data race (thread-safe Win32 convert)

WCHAR2MB and MB2WCHAR used a process-wide `static std::wstring_convert`,
whose mutable internal state is not thread-safe. httplib runs a worker pool
and the MCP path transcodes UTF-16<->UTF-8 on every request/response, so
concurrent calls raced -> corrupted UTF-8 / sporadic crashes under load.

- Windows: convert via WideCharToMultiByte / MultiByteToWideChar directly,
  with locals only (no shared state). MB2WCHAR now does one allocation
  instead of the previous wstring->u16string double copy.
- Non-Windows: the wstring_convert is now a local (was static), so each
  call owns its converter — also race-free.

Signatures unchanged, so all 24 call sites are untouched. Syntax-checked
with MSVC (cl /Zs /std:c++17 /utf-8 + project defines); full compile in CI.

Closes perf-wstring-convert-race (the critical race). Converting straight
into the 1C manager buffer + a concurrent-load test are documented as a
deferred micro-optimization. Also records the deliberate deferral of
perf-ffi-typed-serde (low ROI / high wire-contract risk).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rust-core embedder: tune ONNX sessions per role (query CPU, bulk device)

The two model instances split reindex vs query by lock but not by CPU: each
session defaulted to all-cores intra-op, so reindex and queries oversubscribed
and fought over cores. Now derive per-role SessionTuning from device +
config.intra_threads:

- query: CPU execution provider + min(2, ncpu) intra-op threads. A single
  short query is latency-bound and DirectML loses to CPU there (host<->device
  copies); a small pool also avoids spin-up cost.
- bulk: the configured device (GPU/DirectML when asked) + the explicit
  intra_threads override, else the remaining cores. bulk + query <= ncpu.

fastembed 5.16 exposes with_intra_threads. cargo check --features fastembed
green; cargo test 86/86. Optional batch-size knob, query-session pool, and
before/after latency (needs a GPU box) are documented as deferred.

Closes perf-onnx-thread-config.

* rust-core: configurable multi-session embedding worker pool (+ 1C control)

Adds an opt-in concurrent bulk-embedding pool, settable from 1C via the
configure payload's `embed_workers` (and a `workers=` launch param):
absent ⇒ 1 (single worker, unchanged default), 0 ⇒ auto (≈ncpu/2), n ⇒ n.

- core.rs: the ingest worker is now N threads sharing one job queue (shared
  mpsc receiver; the lock is held only for the brief dequeue, so N embeds run
  concurrently). Worker thread count = embedder.bulk_concurrency(). shutdown
  sends one Stop per thread and joins all.
- fastembed_embedder.rs: FastEmbedder holds a pool of bulk sessions; the
  worker round-robins onto them (N consecutive cursor values ⇒ N distinct
  sessions ⇒ true 1:1 parallelism). Two correctness fixes found by the
  feature: (1) multi-worker forces CPU for the bulk pool — N concurrent
  DirectML sessions on one GPU error out, and a GPU already parallelizes
  within one session; (2) the ONNX sub-batch shrinks to ≈256/N so N
  concurrent sessions don't multiply peak memory and OOM. `run` now surfaces
  embed errors (eprintln) instead of silently returning zeros — that swallow
  is what hid the DirectML failure.
- embed.rs: Embedder::bulk_concurrency() (default 1).
- lib.rs: parse + echo embed_workers.
- Module.bsl (build-22): `embedperf=<dir>` benchmark — reads every *.feature,
  chunks by scenario, indexes with real embedding in batches, polls until the
  worker drains, reports total time + throughput; `workers=N` and `batch=N`
  launch params; periodic progress lines. Gated — perf=0/no-embedperf keeps
  the normal contour.

Measured on the 7561-scenario corpus (CPU, MiniLM-L6): 1w 536s/14 seg/s,
3w 425s/17, 6w 381s/19 — ~1.4× max (memory-bandwidth bound, not ~Nx).
0 failed after the memory fix. 86/86 cargo test; fastembed builds clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* rust-core: worker-pool perf fixes from pool review

Seven fixes to the multi-session embedding pool:

- Pin each worker thread to its own model session (Embedder::
  embed_passages_at(slot)) instead of a shared round-robin cursor: with
  uneven job durations the cursor could queue a worker behind a busy
  session while other sessions idled (head-of-line blocking). The cursor
  stays as the fallback for callers without a stable slot; `slot % n`
  guards retired threads against a smaller resized pool.
- Resize the worker thread pool when reconfigure changes the embedder's
  bulk_concurrency: previously the pool was spawned once and a later
  `embed_workers` change silently loaded M sessions driven by the old N
  threads. Old threads get one Stop each (their FIFO queue drains first)
  and are stashed in retired_workers; joins happen only in shutdown(),
  never under the CORE write lock (a worker may be blocked in apply_job
  on that lock — joining there would deadlock).
- Split large ingests into EMBED_JOB_CHUNK(=256)-sized embed jobs so one
  huge index_segments/index_raw spreads across the whole pool instead of
  serializing on one worker. pending_jobs bumps by the chunk count; an
  empty doc still enqueues exactly one job so the collection can flip
  Ready (single helper keeps bump+enqueue from drifting).
- Move O(total text) accept work (kw multisets; raw normalize/offsets/
  chunking) out of the global write lock: prepare_index[_raw] runs
  before the lock in lib.rs, commit_index[_raw] does only id allocation
  + install + enqueue under it; accept_* kept as thin wrappers for tests.
- Cap auto worker count (embed_workers=0) at (ncpu/2).clamp(1,4):
  measured scaling saturates ~1.4x (memory-bandwidth bound), so more
  sessions only waste one model copy of RAM each.
- Clamp explicit intra_threads to the per-session fair share (ncpu/m)
  when m > 1, preserving the no-oversubscription invariant.
- Surface the last embed failure via stats (last_embed_error):
  eprintln! is invisible inside the 1C host process; FastEmbedder now
  records the most recent ort/poisoned-lock error behind
  Embedder::last_error().

Tests: +2 (chunked 600-segment ingest converges with pending_jobs==3;
pool resize 2->4 retires/joins cleanly, both collections Ready).
89/89 cargo test; cargo check --features fastembed clean.

data-discover="true">build</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix: rcore.dll deps resolve beside it (WS2019), RAM cut, load diagnostics

Three fixes for the two field reports:

- "rag_not_installed although the file IS there" on Windows Server 2019:
  rcore.dll hard-imports DirectML.dll, which is in-box only since
  Windows 10 1903 (Server 2019 = 1809), and plain LoadLibrary resolves
  dependents from the EXE dir, not the DLL dir - so the bundled copy
  was ignored and the load silently failed. Now LoadLibraryExW with
  LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_DLL_LOAD_DIR|DEFAULT_DIRS, and the bundle ships
  the official DirectML redistributable (NuGet 1.15.4, supports 1709+)
  instead of the build host''s System32 copy.
- Silent degradation is gone: when rcore.dll EXISTS but fails to load,
  tools and RagDispatch answer rag_load_failed with the Win32 code and
  a hint (126 = missing DirectML/VC++ runtime, 193 = bitness, 1114 =
  dep init failed); GetStatus gains rag_available + rag_load_error.
- 8 GB RAM on a 150-scenario index: the plugin passed embed_workers=0
  (auto = up to 4 bulk sessions, each a full model copy ~1-1.5 GB).
  Now embed_workers=1 -> bulk+query = 2 copies, ~1.5-2 GB. Docs explain
  the memory model (no hard quota in the core; index itself is tiny).

Smoke T10 now asserts rag_available/rag_load_error in GetStatus for
both stages: 84 checks green. Bundles + .epf rebuilt.

class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="rcore: lead-pull + header-prepend chunking for index_raw

Two optional chunk_cfg knobs, both only active alongside boundary_regex and
both default-off (byte-identical chunking when unused):

- boundary_lead_regex: a line matching it immediately above a boundary (with
  interleaved blanks) is pulled INTO the boundary's chunk, not left in the
  previous one. Lets a Gherkin scenario's leading @tags/#comments stay with
  that scenario instead of bleeding into the prior chunk.
- prepend_header: the document's pre-first-boundary header (feature title +
  description + Background above the first scenario) is capped to ~200 tokens
  and prepended to every later chunk's EMBED text only. Stored text, line
  ranges and kw_counts stay pure source, so grep/get_segment are unchanged;
  only the embedding gains feature context.

Invalid boundary_lead_regex fails the call with bad_pattern before any state
change, same as boundary_regex.

Tests: 106 mock (5 new: lead-pull, header-into-embed-only, parity, invalid
lead e2e, lead+header source-purity e2e).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" class="Link--secondary" href="/DitriXNew/MCP-DB-Client/commit/707c2293b6819093c4f99fa011fbaa6af3e57c03">rcore: lead-pull + header-prepend class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="Add takeScreenshot feature and UI

Introduce a screenshot capture feature for 1C:Enterprise.

- Add a ScreenCapture module (src/ScreenCapture.cpp, ScreenCapture.h) that enumerates visible windows for a PID, captures them via PrintWindow+DWM/GDI+, encodes images as JPEG or PNG, and returns a JSON payload with base64 image data and window metadata.
- Integrate capture API into the native add-in (HttpServerComponent): AddFunction TakeScreenshot and GetProcessId, include new sources in CMakeLists and link gdiplus/ole32/dwmapi on Windows.
- Add client-side/UI support: extend Form.xml and Module.bsl to provide screenshot controls, navigation, and BSL handlers. Register two MCP tools: takeScreenshot and testScreenshot; implement handlers to produce MCP multi-part content (text + image items).
- Update packaging script to support Compress-Archive via powershell on Windows when zip/7z are absent.
- Bump add-in version to 1.4.0 and update the binary template (Template.bin) accordingly.
- Add unit tests to validate MCP image result format and expected capture JSON structure.

The feature supports pid=0 (current process), format="jpeg" (default) or "png", and a quality parameter for JPEG. Errors (no windows, encoder missing) are returned as JSON error messages." class="Link--secondary" href="/DitriXNew/MCP-DB-Client/commit/a04c9e5f8814df9aaaa4630e17800c906787cccf">Add takeScreenshot feature and UI</a></div></div></td><td><div class="react-directory-commit-age"><relative-time tense="past" datetime="2026-03-23T22:53:23.000Z" title="Mar 23, 2026, 10:53 PM UTC">Mar 23, Introduce a new 'query' MCP tool and extend existing tooling: allow 'execute' to run in the thin-client or server context (new 'location' param), implement server-side query execution that returns results as a GitHub-flavored Markdown table, and add parameter casting and reference helpers. Update docs (README.md, TESTING.md) to describe autostart, the new query tool, and client execution. Add test output files (test/build-log.txt, test/server-session/onopen-trace.txt) and remove a template entry from vanessa-plugin/MCPRagSearch.xml; also adjust .gitignore to no longer mask the embedded Template.bin path. class="markdown-body entry-content container-lg" itemprop="text"><div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">http1c — MCP Server Framework for 1C:Enterprise</h1><a id="user-content-http1c--mcp-server-framework-for-1centerprise" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: http1c — MCP Server Framework for 1C:Enterprise" href="#http1c--mcp-server-framework-for-1centerprise"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>http1c</strong> is a framework for building <a href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/" rel="nofollow">Model Context Protocol (MCP)</a> servers from 1C:Enterprise. It provides a native component (DLL) that handles the MCP transport layer and a reference 1C data processor that demonstrates how to implement tools, resources, and prompts.</p>
<p dir="auto">Use it as a template to expose any 1C business logic — catalogs, documents, reports, calculations — to AI applications like VS Code Copilot, Claude Desktop, and other MCP-compatible clients.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Core Concept</h2><a id="user-content-core-concept" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Core Concept" href="#core-concept"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The project is intentionally split into two layers with different responsibilities.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Native component responsibilities</h3><a id="user-content-native-component-responsibilities" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Native component responsibilities" href="#native-component-responsibilities"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The DLL is the MCP engine. It handles protocol and transport details that should not be reimplemented in 1C business code:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>HTTP/SSE transport</li>
<li>JSON-RPC request/response lifecycle</li>
<li>MCP session management</li>
<li>authentication, origin validation, and rate limiting</li>
<li>pagination, notifications, and progress streaming</li>
<li>converting 1C responses into valid MCP replies</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">1C responsibilities</h3><a id="user-content-1c-responsibilities" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 1C responsibilities" href="#1c-responsibilities"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The 1C side owns business logic. A 1C developer should work at the level of tools, resources, and prompts, not at the level of MCP internals:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>describe a tool/resource/prompt in BSL</li>
<li>register it in the component</li>
<li>handle the incoming call in 1C</li>
<li>run any required client-side or server-side 1C logic</li>
<li>return the result or send progress updates</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why the project is designed this way</h3><a id="user-content-why-the-project-is-designed-this-way" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why the project is designed this way" href="#why-the-project-is-designed-this-way"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The goal is to let a 1C developer publish almost any 1C functionality through MCP without having to understand HTTP, JSON-RPC, SSE, session handling, or MCP message formatting.</p>
<p dir="auto">In other words:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>the component knows how to be an MCP server</li>
<li>1C knows what the tool actually does</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">This keeps the integration point simple. To add new functionality, a 1C developer does not need to change the native transport layer. They only add or update 1C definitions and handlers.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Mental model for a 1C developer</h3><a id="user-content-mental-model-for-a-1c-developer" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Mental model for a 1C developer" href="#mental-model-for-a-1c-developer"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">From the 1C side, the workflow is intentionally simple:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Define the MCP object in BSL.</li>
<li>Register it in the component.</li>
<li>Receive the request through <code>ExternalEvent</code>.</li>
<li>Execute arbitrary 1C logic.</li>
<li>Return the final result, and optionally send progress while the operation is running.</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">This means the project is not a fixed set of built-in utilities. It is an MCP transport and protocol layer for 1C, with the actual application behavior defined in 1C code.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Key Features</h2><a id="user-content-key-features" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Key Features" href="#key-features"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Full MCP protocol support</strong> — tools, resources, prompts with <code>listChanged</code> notifications</li>
<li><strong>Streamable HTTP transport</strong> — <code>POST /mcp</code> for requests, <code>GET /mcp</code> for SSE notification stream</li>
<li><strong>Session management</strong> — <code>Mcp-Session-Id</code> header, UUID v4 sessions per spec</li>
<li><strong>Security</strong> — Origin validation (DNS rebinding protection), Bearer token auth, rate limiting</li>
<li><strong>Progress streaming</strong> — SSE-based progress notifications for long-running operations</li>
<li><strong>Pagination</strong> — cursor-based pagination for <code>tools/list</code>, <code>resources/list</code>, <code>prompts/list</code></li>
<li><strong>Tool annotations</strong> — <code>readOnlyHint</code>, <code>destructiveHint</code>, <code>idempotentHint</code>, <code>openWorldHint</code></li>
<li><strong>Output schemas</strong> — typed response contracts for tool results</li>
<li><strong>Dynamic registration</strong> — register/update tools, resources, prompts at runtime from 1C</li>
<li><strong>Built-in semantic search (RAG)</strong> — optional <code>search</code> / <code>grep</code> / <code>get_segment</code> / <code>list_collections</code> tools backed by a Rust search core (<code>rcore</code>) with dense/keyword/hybrid retrieval (see <a href="#search-subsystem-rag">Search Subsystem</a>)</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Architecture</h2><a id="user-content-architecture" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Architecture" href="#architecture"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<ol dir="auto">
<li>A 1C form loads the native add-in and starts the HTTP server.</li>
<li>An MCP client connects to <code>http://localhost:PORT/mcp</code>.</li>
<li>The native component handles protocol-level messages (initialize, tools/list, etc.).</li>
<li>Business logic requests (tools/call, resources/read, prompts/get) are forwarded to 1C via <code>ExternalEvent</code>.</li>
<li>The 1C module processes the request and sends results back through <code>SendResponse</code>.</li>
<li>The DLL wraps the result in a JSON-RPC response and returns it to the client.</li>
</ol>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">4. Open in 1C</h3><a id="user-content-4-open-in-1c" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 4. Open in 1C" href="#4-open-in-1c"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>Open the <code>http1c.epf</code> data processor in your 1C infobase. The MCP server
<strong>starts automatically on open</strong> (attaches the component, registers the tool /
resource / prompt catalogs, and begins listening on port <code>8888</code> by default).</li>
<li>To restart it on a different port, set the port field and click <strong>Connect</strong>.
(The <code>Connect</code> button remains available for manual control.)</li>
<li>Configure your MCP client to connect to <code>http://localhost:PORT/mcp</code>.</li>
</ol>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">5. VS Code configuration</h3><a id="user-content-5-vs-code-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 5. VS Code configuration" href="#5-vs-code-configuration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Add to your <code>.vscode/mcp.json</code>:</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Without authentication:</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"servers"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"1c-mcp-server"</span>: {
      <span class="pl-ent">"type"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>sse<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"url"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>http://localhost:8888/mcp<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
    }
  }
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="{
  "servers": {
    "1c-mcp-server": {
      "type": "sse",
      "url": "http://localhost:8888/mcp"
    }
  }
}" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><strong>With Bearer token authentication:</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-json notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>{
  <span class="pl-ent">"servers"</span>: {
    <span class="pl-ent">"1c-mcp-server"</span>: {
      <span class="pl-ent">"type"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>sse<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"url"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>http://localhost:8888/mcp<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"headers"</span>: {
        <span class="pl-ent">"Authorization"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Bearer ${input:mcpToken}<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>
      }
    }
  },
  <span class="pl-ent">"inputs"</span>: [
    {
      <span class="pl-ent">"id"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>mcpToken<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"type"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>promptString<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"description"</span>: <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>Bearer token for the 1C MCP server<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>,
      <span class="pl-ent">"password"</span>: <span class="pl-c1">true</span>
    }
  ]
}</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="{
  "servers": {
    "1c-mcp-server": {
      "type": "sse",
      "url": "http://localhost:8888/mcp",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer ${input:mcpToken}"
      }
    }
  },
  "inputs": [
    {
      "id": "mcpToken",
      "type": "promptString",
      "description": "Bearer token for the 1C MCP server",
      "password": true
    }
  ]
}" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

</svg>
    </clipboard-copy>

<p dir="auto">When using the <code>${input:...}</code> syntax, VS Code will prompt for the token each time the MCP server connection starts. The entered value is masked as a password.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Important:</strong> The token in VS Code must match the value set on the 1C side. If the server has no token configured (empty string), authentication is disabled and no <code>headers</code> are needed. If a token is set on the server but VS Code sends no <code>Authorization</code> header, the server responds with HTTP 401 and VS Code may try to start an OAuth flow — this is not supported; use the <code>headers</code> approach above instead.</p>
</blockquote>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">How to Build Your Own MCP Server from 1C</h2><a id="user-content-how-to-build-your-own-mcp-server-from-1c" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: How to Build Your Own MCP Server from 1C" href="#how-to-build-your-own-mcp-server-from-1c"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The reference data processor (<code>http-1c-dp</code>) is a working example. Use it as a starting point:</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Registering Tools</h3><a id="user-content-registering-tools" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Registering Tools" href="#registering-tools"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Tools are executable functions that AI clients can invoke. Define them as JSON structures and register with the component:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-bsl notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c">// Create a tool definition</span>
<span class="pl-v">Tool</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> NewTool(<span class="pl-s">"myTool"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"Description of what this tool does"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
AddToolParam(Tool<span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"paramName"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"string"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"Parameter description"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
AddToolAnnotations(Tool<span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">True</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>  <span class="pl-c">// readOnly, safe</span>

<span class="pl-c">// Define output schema (optional, helps clients validate responses)</span>
<span class="pl-v">Schema</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> NewOutputSchema()<span class="pl-k">;</span>
AddOutputProperty(Schema<span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"result"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"string"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"Result description"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
SetToolOutputSchema(Tool<span class="pl-k">,</span> Schema)<span class="pl-k">;</span>

<span class="pl-c">// Register all tools</span>
<span class="pl-v">Tools</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">New</span> Array<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Tools.Add(Tool)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Await Component.RegisterToolsAsync(SerializeToJson(Tools))<span class="pl-k">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="// Create a tool definition
Tool = NewTool("myTool", "Description of what this tool does");
AddToolParam(Tool, "paramName", "string", "Parameter description");
AddToolAnnotations(Tool, True);  // readOnly, safe

// Define output schema (optional, helps clients validate responses)
Schema = NewOutputSchema();
AddOutputProperty(Schema, "result", "string", "Result description");
SetToolOutputSchema(Tool, Schema);

// Register all tools
Tools = New Array;
Tools.Add(Tool);
Await Component.RegisterToolsAsync(SerializeToJson(Tools));" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>
</svg>

    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>

<p dir="auto">Handle the tool call in the <code>ExternalEvent</code> handler:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-bsl notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">&AtClient</span>
Async <span class="pl-k">Procedure</span> <span class="pl-en">ExternalEvent</span>(<span class="pl-v">Source</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-v">Event</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-v">Data</span>)
    <span class="pl-k">If</span> Source <span class="pl-k"><></span> <span class="pl-s">"HttpServer"</span> <span class="pl-k">Then</span> <span class="pl-k">Return</span><span class="pl-k">;</span> <span class="pl-k">EndIf</span><span class="pl-k">;</span>
    <span class="pl-k">If</span> Event <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"ToolCall"</span> <span class="pl-k">Then</span> ProcessToolCall(Data)<span class="pl-k">;</span> <span class="pl-k">EndIf</span><span class="pl-k">;</span>
<span class="pl-k">EndProcedure</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="&AtClient
Async Procedure ExternalEvent(Source, Event, Data)
    If Source <> "HttpServer" Then Return; EndIf;
    If Event = "ToolCall" Then ProcessToolCall(Data); EndIf;
EndProcedure" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Registering Resources</h3><a id="user-content-registering-resources" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Registering Resources" href="#registering-resources"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Resources provide contextual data to AI clients (metadata, file contents, etc.):</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-bsl notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-v">Resource</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">New</span> Structure<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Resource.Insert(<span class="pl-s">"uri"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"1c://metadata/catalogs"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Resource.Insert(<span class="pl-s">"name"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"1C Catalogs"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Resource.Insert(<span class="pl-s">"description"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"List of all catalog metadata objects"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Resource.Insert(<span class="pl-s">"mimeType"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"application/json"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>

<span class="pl-v">Resources</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">New</span> Array<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Resources.Add(Resource)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Await Component.RegisterResourcesAsync(SerializeToJson(Resources))<span class="pl-k">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="Resource = New Structure;
Resource.Insert("uri", "1c://metadata/catalogs");

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Registering Prompts</h3><a id="user-content-registering-prompts" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Registering Prompts" href="#registering-prompts"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Prompts are reusable interaction templates:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-bsl notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-v">Prompt</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">New</span> Structure<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Prompt.Insert(<span class="pl-s">"name"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"analyzeData"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Prompt.Insert(<span class="pl-s">"description"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"Prompt for analyzing 1C data"</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>

<span class="pl-v">PromptArgs</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">New</span> Array<span class="pl-k">;</span>
<span class="pl-v">Arg</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">New</span> Structure(<span class="pl-s">"name,description,required"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"topic"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-s">"Analysis topic"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> <span class="pl-c1">False</span>)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
PromptArgs.Add(Arg)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Prompt.Insert(<span class="pl-s">"arguments"</span><span class="pl-k">,</span> PromptArgs)<span class="pl-k">;</span>

<span class="pl-v">Prompts</span> <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-c1">New</span> Array<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Prompts.Add(Prompt)<span class="pl-k">;</span>
Await Component.RegisterPromptsAsync(SerializeToJson(Prompts))<span class="pl-k">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="Prompt = New Structure;
Prompt.Insert("name", "analyzeData");
Prompt.Insert("description", "Prompt for analyzing 1C data");

PromptArgs = New Array;
Arg = New Structure("name,description,required", "topic", "Analysis topic", False);
PromptArgs.Add(Arg);
Prompt.Insert("arguments", PromptArgs);

Prompts = New Array;
Prompts.Add(Prompt);
Await Component.RegisterPromptsAsync(SerializeToJson(Prompts));" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">Handle prompt gets via <code>"PromptGet"</code> events.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Dynamic Updates</h3><a id="user-content-dynamic-updates" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Dynamic Updates" href="#dynamic-updates"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Call <code>RegisterToolsAsync()</code> / <code>RegisterResourcesAsync()</code> / <code>RegisterPromptsAsync()</code> again at any time with an updated list. The component will automatically send <code>notifications/tools/list_changed</code> (or equivalent) to all connected MCP clients.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Security Configuration</h3><a id="user-content-security-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Security Configuration" href="#security-configuration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The component supports optional Bearer token authentication. When a token is set, every HTTP request must include the <code>Authorization: Bearer <token></code> header or it will be rejected with HTTP 401.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-bsl notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c">// Enable authentication — all requests must include Authorization: Bearer my-secret-token</span>
Component.AuthToken <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-s">"my-secret-token"</span><span class="pl-k">;</span>

<span class="pl-c">// Disable authentication — any request is accepted</span>
Component.AuthToken <span class="pl-k">=</span> <span class="pl-s">""</span><span class="pl-k">;</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="// Enable authentication — all requests must include Authorization: Bearer my-secret-token
Component.AuthToken = "my-secret-token";

// Disable authentication — any request is accepted
Component.AuthToken = "";" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">
    <path d="M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z"></path><path d="M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z"></path>
</svg>
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none">
    <path d="M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z"></path>
</svg>
    </clipboard-copy>
  </div></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>How it works:</strong></p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Server token</th>
<th>Client header</th>
<th>Result</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Empty (default)</td>
<td>None needed</td>
<td>All requests accepted</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>"my-secret"</code></td>
<td><code>Authorization: Bearer my-secret</code></td>
<td>Request accepted</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>"my-secret"</code></td>
<td>Missing or wrong token</td>
<td>HTTP 401 Unauthorized</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Changing the token at runtime:</strong> You can set or clear <code>AuthToken</code> while the server is running. The change takes effect immediately for all new requests — no restart needed.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>VS Code note:</strong> If the server returns 401, VS Code may attempt an OAuth 2.0 PKCE authorization flow (redirecting to <code>/authorize</code>). This is <strong>not supported</strong> by the component. Always configure the token in <code>.vscode/mcp.json</code> via the <code>headers</code> field (see <a href="#5-vs-code-configuration">VS Code configuration</a> above).</p>
<p dir="auto">The component also enforces:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Origin validation</strong> — only requests from <code>localhost</code> / <code>127.0.0.1</code> / VS Code origins are accepted</li>
<li><strong>Rate limiting</strong> — token-bucket algorithm (60 burst, 20/sec)</li>
<li><strong>Session management</strong> — <code>Mcp-Session-Id</code> assigned on initialize, validated on subsequent requests. A request presenting an <strong>unknown</strong> session id is not rejected with 404 — the session is transparently <strong>resurrected</strong> under the presented id, so a server restart does not strand connected MCP clients that never re-initialize (logged as <code>MCP: unknown session ... resurrected (server restart?)</code>)</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Native Component API</h2><a id="user-content-native-component-api" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Native Component API" href="#native-component-api"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Methods exposed to 1C (English / Russian names). All of these are callable
asynchronously from BSL via the platform-generated <code>BeginCalling<Name></code> wrappers
(<code>BeginCallingStartListen</code>, …):</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Method</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>StartListen(port)</code> / <code>НачатьПрослушивание</code></td>
<td>Start the HTTP server on the given port</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>StopListen()</code> / <code>ОстановитьПрослушивание</code></td>
<td>Stop the server and unblock all pending requests</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>SendResponse(json)</code> / <code>ОтправитьОтвет</code></td>
<td>Send the final response for a pending request</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>SendProgress(id, progress, total, message)</code> / <code>ОтправитьПрогресс</code></td>
<td>Send a progress notification for a pending request</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>ApplyConfig(json)</code> / <code>ПрименитьНастройки</code></td>
<td><strong>Async-safe config sink</strong> (see below). Applies any subset of <code>logging_enabled</code>, <code>log_path</code>, <code>timeout</code>, <code>tools_json</code>, <code>resources_json</code>, <code>prompts_json</code>, <code>auth_token</code> in one call</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>GetStatus()</code> / <code>ПолучитьСтатус</code></td>
<td><strong>Async-safe</strong> read of the status JSON (same payload as the <code>Status</code> property)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>RagDispatch(method, payload)</code> / <code>RagВыполнить</code></td>
<td>Drive the Rust search core (<code>rcore</code>) — see <a href="#search-subsystem-rag">Search Subsystem</a></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>TakeScreenshot(pid, format, quality, grayscale)</code> / <code>СделатьСкриншот</code></td>
<td>Capture windows of a process as base64 images</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>GetProcessId()</code> / <code>ПолучитьИдентификаторПроцесса</code></td>
<td>Return the current 1C process id</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Configuration: async methods vs. synchronous properties</h3><a id="user-content-configuration-async-methods-vs-synchronous-properties" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Configuration: async methods vs. synchronous properties" href="#configuration-async-methods-vs-synchronous-properties"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The component also exposes its configuration as <strong>properties</strong> (<code>LoggingEnabled</code>,
<code>LogPath</code>, <code>Timeout</code>, <code>Tools</code>, <code>Resources</code>, <code>Prompts</code>, <code>AuthToken</code>, <code>Status</code>,
<code>Version</code>). These still exist, but reading or writing an AddIn property is a
<strong>synchronous</strong> platform call.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto">In an <strong>async-only infobase</strong> — i.e. one where <em>"synchronous extension and
add-in calls"</em> are disabled (the modern 1C default) — every <code>Component.Prop = …</code>
assignment and every <code>… = Component.Prop</code> read throws
<strong><code>Cannot call synchronous methods on the client!</code></strong>. 1C provides <strong>no async
property accessors</strong> (there is no <code>BeginSetProperty</code>), so configuration cannot
go through properties at all in that mode.</p>
</blockquote>
<p dir="auto">That is why all configuration is funneled through the <strong>async <code>ApplyConfig</code>
method</strong> and all status reads through the <strong>async <code>GetStatus</code> function</strong>. The
reference form (<code>Module.bsl</code>) uses only these — it never touches a property on
the client. The properties are retained for backward compatibility and for
infobases that still allow synchronous calls.</p>

</tr>
<tr>

<td>Bearer token (empty = no auth)</td>

<p dir="auto">Every field is optional — <code>ApplyConfig</code> only touches the keys you send, so it
doubles as a "set just the logging flag" call or a full one-shot configuration.</p>
